title: External calibration (ExtCal) workflow
text: Prior peak integration, peak boundaries need defined either manually via simple algorithm based given minimum peak height. Modified polynomial fitting implemented estimating baseline. Therefore, excerpt pre-processed data evaluated avoid impact signal fluctuations. time window can adjusted correction factor cf determines number data points used around detected peak. default, linear fitting computed. However, parameter deg allows use specified degrees polynomial fitting. example, opted manual peak definition due additional signals present time scan. Peak data collected data.frame information analyte mass transferred function tab_cali(). Generate baseline data. following code plot processed time scans selected calibration standards. grey vertical lines mark peak integration range. baseline drawn [color].  Peak areas plotted analyte mass. Calibration parameter obtained linear regression provided output data.frame.
code: ps <- rep(145, length(cali_pro)) pe <- seq(180, 230, length.out=length(cali_pro)) cf <- 50  cali_peaks <- get_peakdata(   cali_pro,    int_col = int_col,   PPmethod = "Peak (manual)",    peak_start = ps,    peak_end = pe )  cali_peaks <- tab_cali(peak_data = cali_peaks, wf = wf, std_info = seq(0,50,10)) gt::gt(cali_peaks) cali_BL <- lapply(1:length(cali_pro), function(i) {   flt <- (min(which(cali_pro[[i]][,time_col]>=ps[i]))-cf):(max(which(cali_pro[[i]][,time_col]<=pe[i]))+cf)   ETVapp:::blcorr_col(     df = cali_pro[[i]][flt, c(time_col, int_col)],     nm = int_col,      BLmethod = "modpolyfit",     rval = "baseline",      amend = "_BL"   ) }) par(mfrow=c(2,3)) for (i in 1:6) {   plot(cali_pro[[i]], type="l",         ylab = "Intensity [cps]", xlab = "Time [s]", main = paste(cali_peaks[i,6], "ng"))   lines(x = cali_BL[[i]][,c(time_col)], y = cali_BL[[i]][,3], col = "blue")   abline(v=cali_peaks[i,2:3], col=grey(0.8)) } cm <- calc_cali_mod(df = cali_peaks[,c(6,4)], wf = wf) par(mfrow=c(1,1)) plot(cali_peaks[,c(6,4)]) abline(a = cm[1,3], b = cm[1,1]) gt::gt(cm)

title: On-line isotope dilution mass spectrometry (oIDMS) workflow
text: Import process spike containing sample measurement file using workflow “oIDMS”. Correcting ratio column “R_m” provides mass bias corrected values “R_corr”. implemented IDMS equation mass flow calculation adapted Rottmann Heumann. minimum isotope ratio required determined ratio natural isotope abundances. result table obtained consequent peak evaluation. View mass flow diagram check integration mass flow peak(s).
code: samp_imp <- td[["Samples"]]  samp_ion <- lapply(1:length(samp_imp), function(i) {   x <- process_data(data = samp_imp[[i]], wf = wf, c1 = iso1, c2 = iso2, fl = 5)   x[,"R_corr"] <- x[,"R_m"] * mean(K)   return(x) }) str(samp_ion[[1]]) #> 'data.frame':    754 obs. of  5 variables: #>  $ Time  : num  0.032 0.22 0.408 0.595 0.783 ... #>  $ 117Sn : num  2438466 2339865 2227398 2268996 2471078 ... #>  $ 122Sn : num  2900 2333 2167 2800 2767 ... #>  $ R_m   : num  841 1003 1028 810 893 ... #>  $ R_corr: num  954 1137 1166 919 1013 ...  sample_mass <- c(1.0119, 0.9042, 0.9151) result_df <- ldply_base(1:length(samp_ion), function(i) {   x <- samp_ion[[i]][,c("Time","R_corr")]   x[,"mf_s"] <- calc_massflow(     x = x[,"R_corr"],      n_trans = n_trans[,4],      As_iso1 = abnd1, As_iso2 = abnd2,      Asp_iso1 = 91.06, Asp_iso2 = 0.08,      V_fl = 0.0075, c_sp = 19581.71, DF = 20   )   pk <- get_peakdata(x, int_col = "mf_s", PPmethod = "Peak (manual)", peak_start = ps, peak_end = 100)   tab_result(pk, wf = wf, K = K, amae = pk[,4], mass_fraction2 = 1, sample_mass = sample_mass[i]) }) #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary. #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary. #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary.  gt::gt(result_df) Asp_iso1 <- 91.06 Asp_iso2 <- 0.08 V_fl <- 0.0075 c_sp <- 19581.71 DF <- 20  mf_sp <- 0.0075 * n_trans[,4] * (1 / 60) * (c_sp / DF)  mf_data <- lapply(1:length(samp_ion), function(i) {  mf_s <- mf_sp * ((Asp_iso1 - (samp_ion[[i]][,"R_corr"] * Asp_iso2)) / ((abnd2 * samp_ion[[i]][,"R_corr"]) - abnd1))   x <- cbind(samp_ion[[i]][,c("Time","R_corr")], mf_s) })   mf_BL <- lapply(1:length(mf_data), function(i) { flt <- (min(which(mf_data[[i]][,time_col]>=ps))-cf):(max(which(mf_data[[i]][,time_col]<=pe))+cf)   ETVapp:::blcorr_col(     df = mf_data[[i]][flt,c(time_col,"mf_s")],     nm = "mf_s",      BLmethod = "modpolyfit",     rval = "baseline",      amend = "_BL") })   par(mfrow=c(1,3)) for (i in 1:length(mf_BL)){   plot(x = mf_data[[i]][,"Time"], y = mf_data[[i]][,"mf_s"], type="l", ylab="Mass flow [pg/s]", xlab="Time [s]")   lines(x = mf_BL[[i]][,c("Time")], y = mf_BL[[i]][,3], col = "blue")   abline(v=c(result_df[i,1:2]), col=grey(0.8)) }

title: On-line isotope dilution mass spectrometry (oIDMS) workflow
text: Inserting spike aerosol sample vaporization may lead loss regard sample aerosol. included calculation transport efficiency n_trans particle size method using nano particle standard correct effect. Equations data processing steps included package based Excel sheet RIKILT single particle calculation tool (version 2) published article. detailed tutorial single particle measurements, view standard operating procedure. package provides measurement data ionic solutions establish linear calibration curve nano particle standard known size. Import files form sp_ionic. mean signal selected time window obtained via get_peakdata()-function using peak picking method “mean_signal”. tab_cali() collects data.frame standard concentrations. Calibration parameter obtained linear regression. Signal curves ionic measurements resulting calibration curve plotted :   determine limit particle detection (LFD), import single particle data plot signal distribution. LFD shown red. Output parameter single particle analysis obtain calc_transeff() histogram particle size distribution provided following code.
code: spion_imp <- td[["sp_ionic"]] str(spion_imp[[1]]) #> 'data.frame':    20355 obs. of  2 variables: #>  $ Time : num  0.006 0.009 0.012 0.015 0.018 0.021 0.024 0.027 0.03 0.033 ... #>  $ 197Au: num  143249 118398 141905 137537 136865 ...  sp_cali <- get_peakdata(spion_imp, int_col = "197Au", PPmethod = "mean signal", peak_start = 0.006, peak_end = 60)  conc_ion <- c(20, 50, 100, 200, 500) sp_cali <- tab_cali(peak_data = sp_cali, wf = "oIDMS", std_info = conc_ion) gt::gt(sp_cali) cali_lm <- calc_cali_mod(df = sp_cali[,c(5,4)], wf = "oIDMS") gt::gt(cali_lm) par(mfrow=c(1,5)) for (i in 1:min(length(spion_imp), 10)) {   ylim <- c(0, max(sapply(spion_imp, function(x) {max(x[,2])})))   plot(spion_imp[[i]], type="l", main = sp_cali[i,5], ylab="Intensity [cps]", ylim=ylim)   abline(v=sp_cali[i,2:3], col=grey(0.8)) } cm <- calc_cali_mod(df = sp_cali[,c(5,4)], wf = "oIDMS") plot(sp_cali[,c(5,4)]) abline(a = cm[1,3], b = cm[1,1]) gt::gt(cm) sp_data <- ETVapp::ETVapp_testdata[["oIDMS"]][["sp_particle"]][[1]]  # plot_signal_distribution(x = sp_data[,2], style="counts")  time_col <- "Time" anlt <- "197Au" t_fltr <- 60 LFD <- 20000  if (!is.null(t_fltr) && is.numeric(t_fltr) && t_fltr>min(sp_data[,time_col], na.rm=TRUE)) {   message("calc_transeff(): remove data with ", time_col, " > ", t_fltr)   sp_data_flt <- sp_data[sp_data[,time_col]<t_fltr,] } #> calc_transeff(): remove data with Time > 60    sig_data <- table(sp_data_flt[,anlt]) par(mfrow=c(1,1))   plot(sig_data, ylim = c(0, 10), log = 'x', ylab = "Frequency", xlab = "Intensity [cps]", main = "Signal distribution") #> Warning in xy.coords(x, y, xlabel, ylabel, log): 1 x value <= 0 omitted from #> logarithmic plot abline(v=LFD, col=2, lwd=3) V_fl <- 0.0075 size <- 60  n_trans <- calc_transeff(   sp_data_flt,    int_col = anlt,   LFD = LFD,    cali_slope = cali_lm[,1],    V_fl = V_fl,    part_mat = c("Au"),   dia_part = size )  gt::gt(n_trans) sp_data <- td[["sp_particle"]][[1]]  plot_particle_diameter(   sp_data[,c(time_col, anlt)], cali_slope = cm[1,1], V_fl = V_fl, part_mat = "Au", dia_part = size, LFD = LFD )

title: On-line isotope dilution mass spectrometry (oIDMS) workflow
text: Import process spike containing sample measurement file using workflow “oIDMS”. Correcting ratio column “R_m” provides mass bias corrected values “R_corr”. implemented IDMS equation mass flow calculation adapted Rottmann Heumann. minimum isotope ratio required determined ratio natural isotope abundances. result table obtained consequent peak evaluation. View mass flow diagram check integration mass flow peak(s).
code: samp_imp <- td[["Samples"]]  samp_ion <- lapply(1:length(samp_imp), function(i) {   x <- process_data(data = samp_imp[[i]], wf = "oIDMS", c1 = iso1, c2 = iso2, fl = 5)   x[,"R_corr"] <- x[,"R_m"] * mean(K)   return(x) }) gt::gt(head(samp_ion[[1]])) sample_mass <- c(1.0119, 0.9042, 0.9151) result_df <- ldply_base(1:length(samp_ion), function(i) {#'   x <- samp_ion[[i]][,c("Time","R_corr")]   x[,"mf_s"] <- calc_massflow(x = x[,"R_corr"], n_trans = n_trans[,4], As_iso1 = abnd1, As_iso2 = abnd2, Asp_iso1 = 91.06, Asp_iso2 = 0.08, V_fl = 0.0075, c_sp = 19581.71, DF = 20)   pk <- get_peakdata(x, int_col = "mf_s", PPmethod = "Peak (manual)", peak_start = 70, peak_end = 100, minpeakheight = 1000)   tab_result(pk, wf = "oIDMS", K = K, amae = pk[,4], mass_fraction2 = 1, sample_mass = 1) }) #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary. #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary. #> The minimum isotope ratio is below the required value for IDMS calculation. Increasing spike amount or selection of different isotopes is necessary.  gt::gt(result_df) Asp_iso1 <- 91.06 Asp_iso2 <- 0.08 V_fl <- 0.0075 c_sp <- 19581.71 DF <- 20  mf_sp <- 0.0075 * n_trans[,4] * (1 / 60) * (c_sp / DF)  mf_data <- lapply(1:length(samp_ion), function(i) {  mf_s <- mf_sp * ((Asp_iso1 - (samp_ion[[i]][,"R_corr"] * Asp_iso2)) / ((abnd2 * samp_ion[[i]][,"R_corr"]) - abnd1))   x <- cbind(samp_ion[[i]][,c("Time","R_corr")], mf_s) })   mf_BL <- lapply(1:length(mf_data), function(i) { flt <- (min(which(mf_data[[i]][,time_col]>=ps))-cf):(max(which(mf_data[[i]][,time_col]<=pe))+cf)   ETVapp:::blcorr_col(     df = mf_data[[i]][flt,c(time_col,"mf_s")],     nm = "mf_s",      BLmethod = "modpolyfit",     rval = "baseline",      amend = "_BL") })   par(mfrow=c(1,3)) for (i in 1:length(mf_BL)){   plot(x = mf_data[[i]][,"Time"], y = mf_data[[i]][,"mf_s"], type="l", ylab="Mass flow [pg/s]", xlab="Time [s]")   lines(x = mf_BL[[i]][,c("Time")], y = mf_BL[[i]][,3], col = "blue")   abline(v=c(result_df[i,1:2]), col=grey(0.8)) }

title: On-line isotope dilution mass spectrometry (oIDMS) workflow
text: determine limit detection (LOD) quantification (LOQ), ETVapp package includes ten blank measurements spike. Perform data treatment blank files analog samples files (import, optional smoothing, mass bias correction, mass flow calculation). data.frame containing result data generated following code. Mass flow diagrams blank measurements plotted follows.  LOD LOQ computed three ten times standard deviation calling tab_LOX().
code: blk_imp <- ETVapp::ETVapp_testdata[["oIDMS"]][["Blanks"]]  blk_ion <- lapply(1:length(blk_imp), function(i) {   x <- process_data(data = blk_imp[[i]], wf = "oIDMS", c1 = iso1, c2 = iso2, fl = 5)   x[,"R_corr"] <- x[,"R_m"] * mean(K)   x[,"mf_s"] <- calc_massflow(x = x[,"R_corr"], n_trans = n_trans[1,4], As_iso1 = abnd1, As_iso2 = abnd2, Asp_iso1 = 91.06, Asp_iso2 = 0.08, V_fl = V_fl, c_sp = c_sp, DF = DF)   return(x) })  print(str(blk_ion[[1]])) #> 'data.frame':    755 obs. of  6 variables: #>  $ Time  : num  0.032 0.22 0.408 0.595 0.783 ... #>  $ 117Sn : num  1148182 1237134 1237527 1123988 1229097 ... #>  $ 122Sn : num  2700 1867 1967 2133 1800 ... #>  $ R_m   : num  425 663 629 527 683 ... #>  $ R_corr: num  482 752 714 598 775 ... #>  $ mf_s  : num  0.0419 0.0158 0.0183 0.0278 0.0144 ... #> NULL  LOX_pks <- get_peakdata(pro_data = blk_ion, int_col = "mf_s", PPmethod = "Peak (manual)", peak_start = ps, peak_end = pe, minpeakheight = 1000) LOX_df <- tab_result(LOX_pks, wf = "oIDMS", K = K, amae = LOX_pks[,4], mass_fraction2 = 1, sample_mass = 1)    gt::gt(LOX_df) blk_BL <- lapply(1:length(blk_ion), function(i) { flt <- (min(which(blk_ion[[i]][,time_col]>=ps))-cf):(max(which(blk_ion[[i]][,time_col]<=pe))+cf)   ETVapp:::blcorr_col(     df = blk_ion[[i]][flt,c(time_col,"mf_s")],     nm = "mf_s",      BLmethod = "modpolyfit",     rval = "baseline",      amend = "_BL") })   par(mfrow=c(2,5)) par(mar=c(5,3,0,0)+0.1) for (i in 1:min(length(blk_ion), 10)) {   plot(x = blk_ion[[i]][,"Time"], y = blk_ion[[i]][,"mf_s"], type="l", main = names(blk_ion)[i], ylab="Mass flow", xlab = "Time [s]")   lines(x = blk_BL[[i]][,c("Time")], y = blk_BL[[i]][,3], col = "blue")   abline(abline(v=c(LOX_df[i,1:2]), col=grey(0.8))) } gt::gt(tab_LOX(   x = LOX_df[,4],    wf = "oIDMS" ))
